action.hypothesis: Now that `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` is integrated, an architecture-only run at `K=16, EF=12` can test whether router-guided support selection improves the mid-low-K frontier over the current `lowrank_two_stage_residual` point (`FVU=0.10792168974876404`) without changing optimization or width.
action.summary: Round 19 was rejected on isolation grounds before producing a training result, so the clean follow-up is to execute the already-integrated family as a pure architecture comparison. Run `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` at `K=16, EF=12` with the established low-K signum recipe and no code changes this round.
action.self_review: This fixes the previous policy issue by removing code edits from the round and keeping the experiment to a single primary variable: architecture. The main risk is that the routed variant may need router-strength calibration, but the first priority is obtaining a valid comparable datapoint at the target frontier slot.
notes_to_memory: Round 19 was policy-rejected before execution because code-edit introduction and architecture change were coupled; treat the family as already integrated and schedule a pure architecture-only run next.
Use the exact low-K baseline recipe when evaluating `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` so the comparison against the current `K=16, EF=12` frontier point is clean.
next_hypotheses: If `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` wins at `K=16`, run the same family at `K=24, EF=12` to test transfer along the adjacent frontier slot.
If it is close but worse than baseline, keep the family alive and test a smaller routing perturbation in a later code round.
If it clearly loses at `K=16`, return to `lowrank_two_stage_residual` and resume smaller-K frontier work there.

result.termination_reason: Variable isolation violated: Multiple primary variables: architecture, k, ef, lr
